title: Pasta with Garlic and Oil
description: A classic Italian pasta dish ready in 15 minutes with just a handful of ingredients

blocks.name: Pasta Aglio e Olio
blocks.description: A simple Italian classic. Just a handful of ingredients transforms into something deeply satisfying — perfect for a first proper weeknight dinner.

items.title: Cook the pasta
items.body: Bring a large pot of well-salted water to a boil. Add the pasta and cook according to the packet until al dente — tender but with a slight bite. **Before draining, scoop out 1 cup of the starchy cooking water** and set it aside.

items.title: Toast the garlic
items.body: While the pasta cooks, heat the olive oil in a large frying pan over **medium-low heat**. Add the sliced garlic and red pepper flakes (if using). Cook for 2–3 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the garlic is pale golden and fragrant. Watch it closely — it goes from golden to burnt in seconds.

items.title: Bring it together
items.body: Drain the pasta and add it straight into the pan with the garlic oil. Toss well over low heat. If it looks dry or clumpy, add a splash of the reserved pasta water and keep tossing — the starch helps everything come together into a light, glossy coating.

items.title: Season and serve
items.body: Taste and adjust salt and pepper. Plate immediately and top with chopped fresh parsley and grated parmesan if desired. This dish does not keep well — eat it straight away.

blocks.title: The pasta water is the sauce
blocks.content: The starchy water you saved from the pasta pot is what makes the dish creamy without any cream. Add it a little at a time while tossing — you may not need all of it. The more you make this dish, the better you will get at judging it by eye.
